prerequisites: An Amazon Seller Central or Vendor Central account, since account_type must be set to the type of account you are connecting.

Registration as an SP-API developer and a registered application created in the Solution Provider Portal (Seller Central: Apps and Services > Develop Apps > Add new app client), which yields the App ID (app_id), the LWA client ID (lwa_app_id), and the LWA client secret (lwa_client_secret).
A refresh token (refresh_token) generated by self-authorizing your application for the selling partner account you want to sync.
Knowledge of the SP-API region (region) and AWS environment (aws_environment) your application is registered against, both of which are required config fields.

authMethods.name: Login with Amazon (LWA) OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: The SP-API authenticates using OAuth 2.0 through Login with Amazon (LWA). The connector takes the LWA client ID (lwa_app_id) and LWA client secret (lwa_client_secret) from your registered application, plus a long-lived refresh_token, and exchanges the refresh token at runtime for short-lived access tokens used to call the SP-API. The app_id identifies your registered application.

setupSteps.title: Register as an SP-API developer and create your application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Solution Provider Portal (or in Seller Central go to Apps and Services > Develop Apps) and choose Add new app client to register your application. Completing registration provides the App ID along with the LWA client ID and LWA client secret. Supply these to the connector as app_id, lwa_app_id, and lwa_client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Self-authorize the application to obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Authorize your own application for the selling partner account you want to read. In Seller Central choose Apps and Services > Develop Apps, or in Vendor Central choose Integration > API Integration, or use the Manage Authorizations page in the Solution Provider Portal. Choose Authorize app for the account, and a refresh token appears. Provide it to the connector as refresh_token. Set auth_type if your deployment requires it.

setupSteps.title: Configure account, region, and AWS environment
setupSteps.body: Set account_type to match the account you authorized (Seller or Vendor), set region to the SP-API region your application targets, and set aws_environment to the environment your application is registered against. These three fields are required.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication window and optional tuning fields
setupSteps.body: Optionally set replication_start_date and replication_end_date to bound the synced data, and include_pii if you need personally identifiable information. Other optional fields such as period_in_days, report_options_list, num_workers, and the various rate-limit and retry settings can be left at their defaults unless you need to tune throughput.

sources.title: Usage plans and rate limits in the SP-API
